items.title: Expedition 33 creative director says Zelda: Breath of the Wild is “truly exceptional”
items.summary: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 won numerous awards including Game of the Year at The Game Awards and really captured people’s hearts when it was released. In a recent interview the game’s creative…
items.content:
Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 won numerous awards including Game of the Year at The Game Awards and really captured people’s hearts when it was released. In a recent interview the game’s creative director Guillaume Broche spoke about the iconic The Legend of Zelda franchise and how The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild was the first open world game he truly enjoyed stating that “it’s truly exceptional.” He said that the general sense of exploration at the time was unparalleled and that “it’s an absolute masterclass.”

“It’s truly exceptional. In my opinion, it’s the first open-world game that actually delivered on the promise of an open world – of simply not having a minimap. You have a big map, but you really get that sense of exploration. You see something, and you want to go there.”

And, you can. Or, you can do something else – the world is truly open, in that sense. “Ten thousand things happen along the way, and you get sidetracked. You never end up where you want to go because you keep getting sidetracked by all sorts of things.”

As Broche concludes, “In terms of level design, it’s an absolute masterclass.” I can certainly agree there, as a longtime Zelda fan myself. Breath of the Wild and Tears of the Kingdom are wonderful.

Loading… Related 9 thoughts on “Expedition 33 creative director says Zelda: Breath of the Wild is “truly exceptional”” mightypoo June 30, 2026 at 10:45 pm Reply truly, not tears of the bum though

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 Creator Heaps Praise on Kingdom Hearts II and Tales of Vesperia in New Video
items.summary: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 creator Guillaume Broche praised Kingdom Hearts II as one of his favorite games of all time and named Tales of Vesperia his favorite Tales title.
items.content:
Guillaume Broche, one of the creators of the beloved modern turn-based RPG Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 , was recently featured in a video by the YouTube channel Konbini.

Throughout the lengthy, over-an-hour video, Broche discussed the games that inspired him. Besides the expected foundational titles, such as Breath of the Wild , Journey , Devil May Cry , and Lost Odyssey , there were some standout inclusions like Castlevania: Aria of Sorrow and Kingdom Hearts II .

Broche describes Kingdom Hearts II as a truly exceptional title and one of his top five favorite games of all time. He even considers it one of the best action RPGs ever created, emphasizing the late-game depth. In contrast, he didn’t find Kingdom Hearts III quite as good; for him, it couldn’t recapture what Kingdom Hearts II brought to the table.

He didn’t share any comments about the Final Mix inclusions, the Kingdom Hearts III Re Mind DLC, or the non-numbered games.

In another part of the video, Broche revealed that Tales of Vesperia is his favorite Tales of game, praising its art direction and exceptional gameplay that reminds him of late-game Devil May Cry . Judith’s intricate combos are explicitly mentioned. It’s even one of the games he has beaten the most, having done so six or seven times.

You can view the exact clip of Kingdom Hearts II being mentioned below. Thanks to @KHMLTomorrow for sharing this news.

🌟The Creator of Expedition 33 talks about Kingdom Hearts in a New Interview

I wonder if he's played all the non-numbered games

🔗Full Interview: https://t.co/MsuFYmLf6q pic.twitter.com/byUC4LYIEm

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is available on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC.

Kingdom Hearts Collection [I ~ III] will be released natively for Nintendo Switch 2, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S on October 8, 2026. Kingdom Hearts HD 1.5 + 2.5 ReMIX will also be available on Switch.

Kingdom Hearts IV will launch at an unknown time for Switch 2, PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC via Steam and the Epic Games Store.

Tales of Vesperia: Definitive Edition is currently available on PlayStation 4, Xbox One, Nintendo Switch, and PC via Steam .

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 dev heaps praise on Zelda: Breath of the Wild, calls it an "absolute masterclass"
items.content:
0 lightbox#closeBackground keyup@window->lightbox#closeWithKeyboard" class="hidden animated fadeIn fixed inset-0 overflow-y-auto items-center justify-center z-9999"> Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 has been a massive hit for developer Sandfall Interactive, selling over 5 million units in 5 months. The game is proof-positive that gamers are still very interested in high-profile, turn-based RPGs, and rumors continue to swirl of a Switch 2 port, so hopefully Nintendo fans will get to see what all the fuss is about in the near future.

While we wait for news on that front, Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 creative director Guillaume Broche had some kind words to throw Nintendo’s way, speaking specifically about The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild. In a new episode of Video Game Club, Broche heaped praise on Breath of the Wild, a game he admits he was a little reluctant to dive into when he first fired it up.

“I played Zelda when I was really little on the Game Boy. And the only other Zelda game I’ve played is Breath of the Wild.

Generally speaking, I’m not really into open-world games. It’s literally the first open-world game I’ve ever liked. When I started playing it, I was really reluctant.

It’s truly exceptional. In my opinion, it’s the first open-world game that actually delivered on the promise of an open world – of simply not having a minimap. You have a big map, but you really get that sense of exploration. You see something, and you want to go there. Ten thousand things happen along the way, and you get sidetracked. You never end up where you want to go because you keep getting sidetracked by all sorts of things. In terms of level design, it’s an absolute masterclass.”

Direct an issue or corrections to the author. Abuse/misuse of this feature carries penalties.

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 lead says Zelda: Breath of the Wild was "the first open-world game that actually delivered on the promise of an open world"
items.content:
"It's truly exceptional," says Guillaume Broche

When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here’s how it works .

(Image credit: Nintendo) Copy link Facebook X Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter It's no secret that Breath of the Wild broke Nintendo's usual Legend of Zelda formula with its expansive open-world take on Hyrule – but it didn't just revolutionize the decades-old series.

According to Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 creative director Guillaume Broche, it also transformed the open world genre in its entirety. The RPG lead explains as much in a new episode of Video Game Club via Konbini on YouTube (below), first recalling his introduction to The Legend of Zelda as a child. "I played Zelda when I was really little on the Game Boy," says Broche. "And the only other Zelda game I've played is Breath of the Wild."

Watch On This sets the director apart from the rest of Sandfall Interactive, as he points out "we have a lot of big Zelda fans on the team" – but he was impressed by Breath of the Wild, to say the least. "Generally speaking, I'm not really into open-world games," as Broche describes. "It's literally the first open-world game I've ever liked." He wasn't sure he would, though. "When I started playing it, I was really reluctant."

He continues, detailing how he went on to "force" himself to play Breath of the Wild because "everyone was saying it was good." And his verdict? "It's truly exceptional. In my opinion, it's the first open-world game that actually delivered on the promise of an open world – of simply not having a minimap. You have a big map, but you really get that sense of exploration. You see something, and you want to go there."

And, you can. Or, you can do something else – the world is truly open, in that sense. "Ten thousand things happen along the way, and you get sidetracked. You never end up where you want to go because you keep getting sidetracked by all sorts of things." As Broche concludes, "In terms of level design, it's an absolute masterclass." I can certainly agree there, as a longtime Zelda fan myself. Breath of the Wild and Tears of the Kingdom are wonderful.

Shigeru Miyamoto decided Zelda dungeons aren't "really that much fun" while making Ocarina of Time The best Zelda games of all-time Stardew Valley creator Eric Barone says Link's Awakening is his favorite Zelda game because it has an "indie" feel Sure, they break free from Nintendo's beloved Zelda formula – but I'd say that's what makes them so brilliant. They serve as the perfect stepping stone into the series' setting (and apparently, open-world games in general), while also presenting veterans with a fresh take on Hyrule. I know that not everyone feels the same here, but they're undeniably gems. Heck, take a look at their Metacritic scores … even compared to other Zelda entries.

I do wonder what Broche will think of the Ocarina of Time remake now – or whether or not he'll play it at all, considering his previous stance on Zelda. Personally, I hope the Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 director gives it a go.

Weekly digests, tales from the communities you love, and more

Baldur's Gate 3 and Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 show "RPGs are as vibrant" now as ever, Obsidian lead says, even if the genre can look "a little tired" sometimes.

After spending years with her head in various fantastical realms' clouds, Anna studied English Literature and then Medieval History at the University of Edinburgh, going on to specialize in narrative design and video game journalism as a writer. She has written for various publications since her postgraduate studies, including Dexerto, Fanbyte, GameSpot, IGN, PCGamesN, and more. When she's not frantically trying to form words into coherent sentences, she's probably daydreaming about becoming a fairy druid and befriending every animal or she's spending a thousand (more) hours traversing the Underdark in Baldur's Gate 3. If you spot her away from her PC, you'll always find Anna with a fantasy book, a handheld video game console of some sort, and a Tamagotchi or two on hand. items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 Patch 1.5.6 Released & Detailed
items.summary: Sandfall Interactive has just released Title Update 1.5.6 for Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 and shared its full patch notes.
items.content:
Sandfall Interactive has just released Title Update 1.5.6 for Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 and shared its full patch notes. So, let’s see what this new patch brings to the table.

According to the devs, the biggest feature of Patch 1.5.6 is support for the Arabic language. Moreover, the devs have fixed some issues that have plagued the game.

For example, it fixes a bug where applying Burn to a stunned enemy allowed that enemy to act immediately after recovering. It also fixes Cleim’s Level 10 Lumina weapon effect, which did not trigger correctly with every area-of-effect skill.

Chromatic Lampmaster’s area-of-effect jump attack now has a sound cue. Sirene’s Widow Ballet now plays a sound effect during its second charm attack. In addition, Martenon’s Lumina effects now work correctly even if Sciel does not take two turns in a row.

The patch also fixes an issue where the Sireso weapon, the Level 4 Lumina effect, and the Powerful buff from Pictos and Luminas could overwrite one another at the start of battle, depending on a character’s position. Finally, it fixes a Photo Mode bug that could cause some characters to appear with misplaced heads during combat.

Sandfall Interactive has also acknowledged a crash that can occur when loading into the Reacher or going out of the Manor. This crash is related to some specific PC configurations. Until the devs fix it, you can work around it by setting ‘Global Illumination’ to anything below ‘Epic’ temporarily.

Like always, Steam will download this update the next time you launch its client. Below, you can also find its full patch notes.

Credits updated (new linguists, testers and fonts)

Fixed an issue where applying burn after an enemy has been stunned causes them to be able to act immediately after recovering from the stun.

Fixed Cleim weapon level 10 Lumina effect that does not trigger correctly on every instance of area of effect skills.

Fixed Martenon’s Lumina effects that do not work if Sciel does not have two consecutive turns.

Fixed an issue where Sireso weapon, Level 4 Lumina, and Powerful buff, from Pictos/Luminas, applied at the start of battle may override each other depending on the character’s position in combat.

Fixed Chromatic Lampmaster’s AOE skill missing a sound cue on the jump attack.

Fixed Sirene’s Widow Ballet missing SFX during second charm attack.

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 New Update 1.500.600 on June 30 Gives More Devs Their Flowers
items.summary: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 has been updated to version 1.5.6 and appears as download 1.500.600 on consoles and adds a few small suff.
items.content:
While Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is already feature-complete as a single-player, narrative-driven RPG, that hasn’t stopped developer Sandfall Interactive from releasing countless updates to add to the experience.

Today, a new game update has been released, and while it’s not a major patch, it’s something game developers — and even some gamers — will appreciate.

Players will be able to download Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 update 1.500.600 for version 1.5.6 today, June 30 , and this adds more developers to the credits section, and also slots in a few bug fixes too.

For the complete info, read on for what Sandfall has to say.

Sandfall has acknowledged a crash that occurs when loading into the Reacher, and has issued a workaround:

According to the developers, the crash only affects certain PC hardware or software configurations.

This only affects the PC version, and we’re hoping a patch for it is released soon.

While nothing has been announced officially, the developers did confirm that more games in the franchise are on the way.

RELATED Microsoft Rejects Reports That PS5 GTA 6 Preorders Are Beating Xbox 8 to 1 In the past few hours, reports claimed that the PS5 version of GTA 6 was getting far more preorders than the Xbox version, with an estimated six-to-one gap.

Stay connected to MP1st and the latest news by following us on Bluesky , X , Facebook , TikTok , YouTube , and Google News .

Father, gamer, games media vet, writer of words, killer of noobs.

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is now available in Arabic!
items.summary: Expeditioners! Following the huge demand from so many of you, we’ve been working super hard to be able to finally announce that Clair Obscur: Expedition 33, is now available with Arabic text! This brings the total number of supported langua
items.content:
Expeditioners!

Following the huge demand from so many of you, we’ve been working super hard to be able to finally announce that Clair Obscur: Expedition 33, is now available with Arabic text!

This brings the total number of supported languages to 20!

With the new Arabic language support, we had to consider additional changes to the game’s UI to ensure a smooth and enjoyable experience for Arabic-speaking players around the world.

Adding Arabic localization has been a true team effort over many months, from the incredible adaptation and localization work carried out by the linguists, to reworking the vast majority of the user interface for right-to-left reading, all the way through localization testing.

Every team involved brought passion and dedication to making this possible, and we couldn’t be more grateful for their work. We hope this update makes the story feel even more personal to our players.

Today’s update also includes some additional fixes and improvements. Read the full patch notes here!

Thank you all so much for your continued support! <3

Tomorrow comes.

~ The Expedition 33 Team

items.title: Expedition 33 June 30 1.5.6 Patch Notes
items.summary: The 1.5.6 Patch for Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 has arrived, bringing with it Arabic text support alongside a handful of fixes and
items.content:
The 1.5.6 Patch for Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 has arrived, bringing with it Arabic text support alongside a handful of fixes and optimizations.

This page acts as a comprehensive breakdown of the 1.5.6 Patch for Expedition 33, including a link to the full Patch Notes.

If you wish to read the Patch Notes in full, you can do so via the official Steam Community Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 1.5.6 Patch Notes post.

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 Receives Update 1.5.6 - Players Advised to Lower Graphics Quality to Avoid Bug
items.summary: Developers continue to support the game and fix various bugs.
items.content:
0 Источник: Sandfall Interactive, Kepler Interactive 30 Jun 13:41 Developers continue to support the game and fix various bugs.

Sandfall Interactive and Kepler Interactive announced the release of update 1.5.6 for Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 at the end of June. It does not add new content but includes several technical improvements, expands the list of supported languages, and more.

The developers stated that they updated the credits for Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 – "new linguists, testers, and font artists were added," and the game now supports Arabic (text translation is available to players).

The developers heard feedback about crashes in some locations. According to them, this issue is related to "certain PC configurations," and they are still working on a fix. As a temporary solution, players are advised to lower graphics settings: set the "Global Illumination" parameter below the "Epic" level.

Update 1.5.6 also fixes several bugs related to effect layering on enemies, missing sound effects, and includes "various minor UI and localization fixes."

The full description of update 1.5.6 for Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is available at the following link.

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 director says Kingdom Hearts 2 is "one of the best action RPGs ever created," and there's "something about that gameplay" he's been chasing "ever since"
items.content:
"Kingdom Hearts 3 isn't bad, but it didn't manage to recapture everything it did in terms of gameplay," says Guillaume Broche

When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here’s how it works .

(Image credit: Square Enix) Copy link Facebook X Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Guillaume Broche, creative director of Clair Obscur: Expedition 33, says Kingdom Hearts 2 is one of the best action RPGs ever created.

Speaking to Konbini on its Jeux Video Club series (below), Broche singles out Kingdom Hearts 2 as one of his "top five games of all time," adding: "You have to admit that Square Enix has managed to create something truly exceptional."

He points to the gameplay in the series, "especially in the second game," which he considers "one of the best action RPG's ever created," while shouting out the late-game combos and fusions you can acquire throughout the adventure.

Watch On "There's really something about that gameplay that I've been looking for ever since Kingdom Hearts 2," he says, adding: "You could spend 150 hours in the late game."

Obviously, there have been games in the series since then, and Broche notes "Kingdom Hearts 3 isn't bad, but it didn't manage to recapture everything it did in terms of gameplay, which was exceptional." He doesn't say if he's played the litany of other titles that are all somehow canon, so maybe Kingdom Hearts 0.2: Birth by Sleep – A Fragmentary Passage would hit the mark for Broche.

Despite playing a key role in Final Fantasy 7, Tetsuya Nomura says "Kingdom Hearts has become my life's work" Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth boss says RPGs like Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 "may have been inevitable" Clair Obscur Expedition 33 head says the volleyball minigame is "easy," then proves it on stage For as much as a breath of fresh air Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 was seen as for many, it's very much a game that proudly wears its inspirations on its sleeve. Of course, the game is heavily inspired by Final Fantasy in more ways than you can count, while Verso's gameplay heavily leans on Devil May Cry – a game which Broche previously uploaded challenge runs of before developing his own game – with the ranking system and pretty much every one of his moves being ripped straight from Vergil. With the blend of action and RPG mechanics, it's not a shocker to hear that Broche is pretty keen on Kingdom Hearts too.

"It's a pure skill issue": Clair Obscur Expedition 33 head says the RPG's notorious volleyball minigame is "easy," then proves it on stage.

Weekly digests, tales from the communities you love, and more

Scott has been freelancing for over four years across a number of different gaming publications, first appearing on GamesRadar+ in 2024. He has also written for the likes of PC Gamer, Eurogamer, VG247, Play, TechRadar, and others. He's typically rambling about Metal Gear Solid, God Hand, or any other PS2-era titles that rarely (if ever) get sequels. items.title: 10 RPGs With Perfect Endings
items.summary: When an RPG finishes on a beautiful note, the way Witcher 3 and Kingdom Hearts did, it cements its place in gaming history forever.
items.content:
clair obscur expedition 33 featured art Image via Sandfall Interactive By Shawn Wilken Published Jun 28, 2026, 8:30 PM EDT Shawn Wilken is an avid anime and manga fan for most of his life, and is best known for his work in journalism spanning that time, working as a credentialed sports writer for LastWordOnSports.com, before transitioning to a gaming and anime writer. Joining CBR, Shawn brings a wealth of knowledge in the anime and manga industry and over two decades of journalism experience. Sign in to your CBR account Add Us On Summary Generate a summary of this story follow Follow followed Followed Like Like Thread Log in Here is a fact-based summary of the story contents: Try something different: Show me the facts Explain it like I’m 5 Give me a lighthearted recap The hallmark of a masterful RPG lies in its ability to stick the landing. When a player invests dozens or even hundreds of hours into a game , a poorly executed climax can diminish the entire journey. Conversely, a flawless conclusion elevates the entire experience, transforming an interactive journey into an unforgettable benchmark.

Crafting a narrative payoff of this caliber requires an extraordinary alignment of world-building, character agency, and structural pacing. To cross these legendary thresholds, developers frequently challenge players with sophisticated mechanics, complex thematic subtext, and punishing gameplay barriers.

Image via CD Projekt Red Developed by CD Projekt Red, this massive open world adventure follows Geralt of Rivia on a deeply personal quest to find his adopted daughter, Ciri. Set in a war-torn continent filled with dark folklore and complex political intrigue, The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt excels at blending intimate character choices with a grand cinematic narrative to set an unmatched experience.

Players navigate a vast landscape where their moral decisions carry heavy weight, influencing everything from local village disputes to the fates of entire empires. The ending is a masterclass in narrative payoff because it completely avoids standard fantasy clichés and instead hinges entirely on the subtle parental choices Geralt makes throughout the journey.

By focusing on Ciri's ultimate agency and her relationship with her adoptive father, the climax ties the entire story together around the core theme of family. Whether Ciri tragically sacrifices herself or returns to live a life of freedom, the resolution feels completely earned, providing profound thematic closure to an epic saga.

The True Pacifist Ending of Undertale, which shows all the characters staring at a sunset together Image via Toby Fox Created by indie developer Toby Fox, this game completely subverts traditional roleplaying conventions by dropping a human child into an underground world populated by monsters. Undertale heavily tracks player behavior, offering drastically different outcomes depending on whether players choose to slaughter every creature or make friends through the unique turn-based Mercy mechanic .

The experience blends witty humor, meta-narrative commentary, and unforgettable character interactions to create a deeply personal journey. The true Pacifist ending stands as a legendary conclusion because it perfectly embraces every single interaction the player had throughout the game.

By resolving conflicts with love instead of violence, players break a multi-generational cycle of hatred between monsters and humans. The emotional climax against Asriel Dreemurr beautifully weaves every character arc together, giving players a deeply earned sense of peace.

Mass Effect 2 ending scene Image via BioWare Developed by BioWare, this sci-fi epic places Commander Shepard in charge of a mismatched crew tasked with a seemingly impossible assault on the mysterious Collectors. The gameplay of Mass Effect 2 centers around deep character recruitment , tactical third-person shooting, and cultivation of intense personal loyalty among a diverse crew of aliens and humans.

Every companion brings a unique background and set of skills, turning the preparation for the final mission into a high-stakes study of leadership and friendship. The legendary Suicide Mission serves as the ultimate culmination of the choices made regarding team loyalty and ship upgrades.

The ending ties the story of Mass Effect 2 together flawlessly by making every single companion's fate depend entirely on the bond they forged with Shepard. Surviving the impossible base raid or dealing with the tragic loss of teammates provides an unforgettable conclusion that perfectly encapsulates the game's core themes of galactic camaraderie and sacrifice.

Cloud Strife beside Zack Fair's lifeless body in Crisis Core: Final Fantasy 7 Reunion Image via Square Enix Square Enix developed this polished remaster to tell the tragic prequel story of Zack Fair , a bright-eyed soldier caught in the corrupt web of the Shinra Electric Power Company. Crisis Core: Final Fantasy 7 Reunion utilizes a fast-paced action combat system layered with a unique slot machine interface that reflects Zack's emotional state and memories on the battlefield.

The journey beautifully expands upon the lore of a beloved universe, fleshing out the motivations of key characters before the events of the main game. While the game itself received mixed reviews, the ending is universally praised for its tragic, poetic beauty.

Knowing Zack's inevitable fate makes his final stand against the Shinra army an emotional bang that ties the entire prequel directly into the opening moments of Final Fantasy 7 . The clever mechanical choice to have the digital mind wave breakdown as Zack loses his memories perfectly fuses gameplay with narrative, leaving an indelible mark as he passes his literal and figurative legacy on to Cloud, telling him to live before taking his final breath.

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 actor reveals the lousy advice he got from Ben Starr
items.summary: It's important to manage your expectations, after all.
items.content:
By Austin Manchester Published Jun 28, 2026, 11:00 AM EDT Interview Rich Keeble is still surprised by how successful Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 has been

Image: Sandfall Interactive/Kepler Interactive Sign in to your Polygon.com account Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 has become so special to players that some fans are sending out wedding invitations to its cast.

That’s according to Rich Keeble , the voice behind Gestral warrior Monoco and plenty of other characters. “I've just had an email forwarded to me that people want to invite us to their weddings and things like that,” he told Polygon over a video interview. “I mean, funny if it was just me turning up and we were like, ‘Oh yeah, typical. Rich Keeble wanted to get a free meal.”

Keeble knew the game would be a good one while he was working on it, but not quite this popular. He’s met “all these lovely people” at conventions who call Expedition 33 the game of the decade and get tattoos to celebrate it. (Not all have extended him a wedding invitation, however.)

Keeble never thought he’d be in a position like this. He came to acting relatively late, in his early 30s. He carved out a niche in voice work with his deep voice and comedic background, while still pursuing on-camera work as “bald ginger guys with glasses, or sometimes not glasses.” He’s still taken by surprise more than a year after Expedition 33 ’s launch, though even the game's success hasn’t quite calmed all the nerves he gets when in the recording booth.

“Looking back, it’s just been crazy with the awards, and then we've just got BAFTA as well, Best Game,” he said. “I still think it hasn't really sunk in.”

Image: Sandfall Interactive/Kepler Interactive Keeble got to meet some of his costars, like Ben Starr (Verso) and Kirsty Rider (Lune) before they started recording, which he describes as unusual: “You don't often know who the cast [members] are until the game is out.”

Like all of us on the outside, Keeble watched Expedition 33 skyrocket in popularity in real time leading up to the game’s April 2025 launch. As developer Sandfall Interactive and publisher Kepler Interactive put out various trailers promoting it (including a Monoco-specific one ), he noticed a steady uptick in the game’s following online.

“I started to get a bit nervous,” Keeble said. He met Starr for coffee and asked for Starr’s take on the growing spotlight. While Starr thought it would do well, he tried to temper Keeble’s expectations, saying some people might not click with the turn-based combat. (Oh, how he was wrong .) According to Keeble, Starr said, “‘I think it'll do well, but I don't think it's going to be a Baldur's Gate 3 -style movement.’”

Starr just wanted to manage expectations, but, “of course, he managed them the wrong way, because the game was a phenomenon,” Keeble said.

Image: Sandfall Interactive/Kepler Interactive While Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is a rather serious game that centers around grief first and foremost, it’s also a very silly game at times. Characters get mime-like outfits complete with baguettes tied to their backs. Gestrals goof about. A journal entry recounts past expeditioners who were naked and jacked .

Keeble’s Monoco brings a much-needed levity to the game, and that’s never more apparent than his introduction . After defeating a Nevron (and yanking its foot away), Verso tries to recruit his Gestral friend. “We are heading for Old Lumière,” Verso says. “OK, bye,” Monaco promptly deadpans and turns away. The two then engage in a very fun back-and-forth where Monoco is ultimately convinced to join because there will be a lot of fighting.

“Actually, Ben did say to me, to be fair, people will clip that and you will see that a lot,” Keeble said. And Starr was right; within a few days of the game being out, Keeble saw a clip on social media of that scene that already had over five million views. “I love the way that even though [Monoco] hasn't got a face, he’s just so expressive still. It's genius.”

“Of course, he managed them the wrong way, because the game was a phenomenon.”

items.title: 5 Masterpiece Turn-Based RPGs Better Than Clair Obscur
items.summary: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is an amazing title, but it's not quite the peak of the turn-based RPG genre.
items.content:
Maelle attacking an enemy in Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 Image via Sandfall Interactive By Sage Ashford Published Jun 27, 2026, 10:45 PM EDT Staff Writer for CBR, Sage Ashford has also written for Comicon as well as other sites such as The Gamer, and has been doing freelance work since 2014, and been working for CBR since 2016. His focus is primarily on spreading the word on obscure anime, comic books, and games whenever possible. Follow him on Twitter @ sageshinigami, or on YouTube @ nolevelcap. Sign in to your CBR account Add Us On Summary Generate a summary of this story follow Follow followed Followed Like Like Thread 16 Log in Here is a fact-based summary of the story contents: Try something different: Show me the facts Explain it like I’m 5 Give me a lighthearted recap When Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 released, many gamers pronounced it as the rebirth of turn-based role-playing games . To be fair, it's an incredible game, and one that deserved to be showered with awards. However, as long-time fans of turn-based games are well aware, Clair Obscur isn't at all the best entry in the genre.

With Clair Obscur being such an amazing game, the games that surpass it are naturally all masterpieces. Regardless of when in the last 30+ years these turn-based RPGs came out, they all had a huge impact, and are all must-play experiences for anyone who enjoyed Expedition 33 at all.

Fire Emblem Awakening key art showing Chrom and the rest of their army jumping into battle Image via Intelligent Systems Every bit of success that Fire Emblem has in the modern era can be traced back to Fire Emblem Awakening . It's not spoken of as much now, but before the 3DS title came out, the Fire Emblem franchise was one of Nintendo's most niche IPs.

However, a shift in art styles, combined with Fire Emblem Awakening being one of the best turn-based games of the 2010s, forced people to finally respect the Fire Emblem franchise . Set two millennia after the events of the original two Fire Emblem games, Fire Emblem: Awakening takes place in the land of Ylisse.

There, an amnesiac strategist named Robin begins helping Chrom, the prince of Ylisse, in his battles against a powerful undead army. Together, they hope to find the power to defeat both the undead and put a stop to the threat of the Fell Dragon Grima with the help of the Divine Dragon Naga's Fire Emblem.

While most games that try to appeal to a more casual audience lose their personality in the process, Fire Emblem Awakening doesn't have that problem. Despite the addition of a "Casual Mode" to allow players not to put their allies at risk, Awakening still offers a great challenge, while adding new systems to keep the gameplay feeling fresh.

Cloud wielding his Buster Sword in battle against a pair of enemies wielding guns in the PS1 version of Final Fantasy VII Image via Square Enix F inal Fantasy VII has stood the test of time for decades now. As one of the best RPGs of the PS1 era , FF7 helped the casual fanbase realize the potential of the genre. Cloud and the rest of Avalanche's battle against both Shinra and Sephiroth is a timeless story, packed with unforgettable moments and themes that feel more relevant now than they did when the game first released.

Final Fantasy VII carries forward the Active Time Battle system introduced on the SNES. With enemies capable of attacking whenever their meter fills, players are punished for being lackadaisical in their play. This subtle shift in gameplay forces players to think a bit faster, pulling them deeper into the combat while still not requiring the twitch reflexes of a more action-focused game.

Though it pre-dates the concept of the modern open-world, Final Fantasy VII also feels open in a way that many RPGs don't. After the initial Midgar section, it really does feel like everything is available to explore, with players able to visit all of the world's varied locales.

A glimpse from above at the sprawling and bustling city of Baldur's Gate within Baldur's Gate 3 Image via Larian Studios A Game of the Year winner itself, Baldur's Gate 3 was a massive, yet welcome surprise when it released. While the game had been in Early Access for some time, initial impressions weren't quite reflective of what it eventually became.

By the time it finally released in 1.0, it became a massive sensation, tapping into what everyone loved about the Dungeons and Dragons tabletop games and merging it with Larian's own brilliant writing . Baldur's Gate 3 starts with a group of people awakening inside a mind flayer ship after being implanted with tadpoles that will transform them all into illithids.

After an attack brings the ship down, the group escapes together in the hopes of discovering a way to cure their transformation before it's too late. While searching for a cure, the group gets pulled into other conflicts around the land, most of which are tied to a mysterious being known as the Absolute.

Baldur's Gate 3 is proof that the best turn-based games can still be just as challenging as any action RPG. With limited resources for battle, every new combat encounter is a question of how good the player can be at outsmarting their enemies. Fortunately, Larian Studios made it possible to approach each battle however the player wants, whether that means raining down fireballs from the skies or covering an area in water and electrifying enemies.

Trails in the Sky 1st Chapter image showing Estelle Bright, Tita Russell, Agate Crosner, Kloe Rinz, and Sieg Image via Nihon Falcom Falcom has kept their Legend of Heroes series going for twenty years now and, to celebrate they decided to remake the original Legend of Heroes: Trails in the Sky . In First Chapter , players join Estelle and Joshua, two teens traveling their home country, Liberl, in the hopes of becoming Bracers. Along the way, the two get pulled into a dangerous national conspiracy, all while figuring out their feelings for one another.

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 x Dispatch Bundle
items.summary: Expeditioners! Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 and Dispatch are now available together in a 10% off bundle! Bundle art by Derek Stratton This is a really special bundle for us - since its release, Dispatch left such an impact on us and immediat
items.content:
Expeditioners!

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 and Dispatch are now available together in a 10% off bundle! 

Bundle art by Derek Stratton

This is a really special bundle for us - since its release, Dispatch left such an impact on us and immediately became one of our favourites.   

Our games are quite different, but we couldn’t help ourselves - and ultimately, we just want more people to discover the powerful stories and artistry behind these games.   

So in the words of our Creative Director, Guillaume Broche: “Play Dispatch, it’s very very cool”

Save 10% off both games by purchasing this bundle!

items.title: 1st Anniversary of Clair Obscur: Expedition 33
items.summary: Expeditioners!! Today marks 1 year since we released Clair Obscur: Expedition 33. This past year has been an incredible journey and so we have a few things to share with you today. To celebrate the occasion, the team put together a special
items.content:
Expeditioners!! 

 

Today marks 1 year since we released Clair Obscur: Expedition 33. This past year has been an incredible journey and so we have a few things to share with you today. 

 

To celebrate the occasion, the team put together a special piece of artwork that we hope you will enjoy! 

8 Million Copies Sold

We're so thrilled, and honestly still quite overwhelmed, to announce that Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 has officially reached 8 million copies sold!

Thank you so so much to every single person who has played, created art, shared, streamed, discussed, and supported this game!!

Game Update: New Haircuts + Patch Notes

We've pushed a fun little update live today: new haircuts are now available in-game, giving the expedition even more style to strut around in! 💅 

For the full list of changes, fixes, and improvements, you can read the patch notes here

Community Emotes

Our community is packed with phenomenally talented artists and we wanted to celebrate that today.  

We commissioned some of our amazing community creators to design official Expedition 33 emotes for you to use in your own channels! 

A massive thank you to IrunoArt, comickergirl (Sarah Leuver), and Bugyakis for their brilliant work on each of these!!

They're FREE to download and use here

Official Baguette Merch

In the mood to add some baguette to your wardrobe? 

We’ve partnered with Orcival and Le Béret Français to bring you the official Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 striped shirt and beret for your cosplays! 🥖    You can purchase the official Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 x Orcival mariniere here

And the official Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 x Le Béret Français beret is available to buy here

On top of that, keep an eye out for a limited number of units of the Monolith Set to be back in stock from our partners at Laced and Pix n' Love soon!! 

And Finally… Cake

It's been a big day for us. Thank you for making it possible. 🎂 

And if you know someone who wants to pick up the game, we're running a 20% sale right now on Steam - As well as some fun merchandise goodies available on our merch page!

Merch Page

From the bottom of our hearts, we’re so thankful to each and every one of you – this has been the best year of our lives, thanks to you. 

 

- The Expedition 33 Team

items.title: Patch v1.5.5 is now LIVE!
items.summary: Expeditioners! Happy 1 Year Anniversary of Clair Obscur: Expedition 33!! The details of this patch are written out below: New content Added 1st Anniversary haircuts for the expedition! Gustave's Anniversary haircut is available at the Gestr
items.content:
Expeditioners!

Happy 1 Year Anniversary of Clair Obscur: Expedition 33!! The details of this patch are written out below:

New content

  • Added 1st Anniversary haircuts for the expedition!

    • Gustave's Anniversary haircut is available at the Gestral Merchant near Stone Wave Cliffs.

      [/*]

    • Verso + Maelle + Lune + Sciel + Monoco's Anniversary haircuts are available at the Gestral Merchant near Grosse Tête on the World Map.

      [/*]
    [/*]

Fixes

  • Fixed an issue where Danseuse outfit for Lune and Sciel made the character menu too obscur.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where it was not possible to open a rest point in various locations when an in-world dialogue was playing.

    [/*]

  • Fixed collision issues in various locations.

    [/*]

  • Adjusted some achievement text for consistency.

    • We've noticed something went wrong after adjusting this, we're investigating now to resolve the issue

      [/*]

    • Fixed the issue! We've readjusted some of the achievement descriptions back to the correct achievements. Thank you for your patience!!

      [/*]
    [/*]

As always, thanks for your support and for your reports via the official Typeform - and most importantly, thank you for all of your support this entire year!! We wish you a wonderful anniversary day and hope you enjoy some cake to celebrate with us!

~ The Expedition 33 Team

items.title: Patch v1.5.3 is live!
items.summary: Expeditioners! Thank you for all of your reports - the details for the fixes in this patch are written out below: Update On Sirene Crash After a thorough internal investigation, the crash that occurred after defeating Sirene can no longer b
items.content:
Expeditioners!

Thank you for all of your reports - the details for the fixes in this patch are written out below:

Update On Sirene Crash

  • After a thorough internal investigation, the crash that occurred after defeating Sirene can no longer be reproduced on our end. If you keep getting this issue, we’d suggest updating your Windows to the latest version. If the issue persists, please submit a report and we’ll continue investigating.

    [/*]

Bug Fixes & Improvements

Photo Mode

  • Fixed facial hair of characters being blurred in the Photo Mode on the close distance if Shading was set to Epic.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in Photo mode that prevented the Inner Cone Angle and Outer Cone Angle options from affecting the shape of the preview light source.

    [/*]

  • Photo Mode's spotlight and point light display has been modified to improve their visibility

    [/*]

Gameplay

  • Fixed an issue where loading a save while picking up a journal could cause that journal to disappear permanently and become impossible to collect again.

    • Missing journals are now also restored retroactively when loading affected saves.

      [/*]
    [/*]

  • Fixed a soft lock that could occur, during the Chromatic Glissando encounter in Sirene’s Dress level, when a dead expeditioner equipped with Shortcut and Second Chance pictos was swallowed.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue on Gestral Beach where using the Rematch option after losing the volleyball minigame could create a blocked save.

    [/*]

  • Fixed a visual issue where characters affected by Inverted would not have the correct colour effect applied to their eyes.

    [/*]

Environment

  • Fixed various environment issues (collisions, misplaced assets, missing or incorrect footsteps SFX) across Verso's Drafts, the World Map, and other levels.

    [/*]

  • Fixed various lighting issues on Steam Deck in Verso’s Drafts, Endless Tower and Stone Wave Cliffs levels.

    [/*]

As always, thanks for your support and for your reports via the official Typeform - We'll continue monitoring the reports coming in and working on future fixes!

~ The Expedition 33 Team
